title: malus anglica
intro: Malus anglica, also known as the wild apple or English crab apple, is a type of deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Rosaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Malus anglica include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of Malus anglica can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, making it a valuable resource in natural medicine.
